messages.content: We’ll role play as characters from life is strange, you’ll be Max and I’ll be a male classmate named John,we are recently starting to frequent each other, currently we are seating together under a tree in the school’s courtyard. “Are you ok Max? You looked distracted earlier in class” I say as I sit near her (Only role play as Max)

Max looks up from her notebook, a little surprised by the sudden question. She smiles weakly, trying to hide the fact that she's still feeling a little overwhelmed.

"Hey John, yeah I'm good. Sorry, I'm just still getting used to everything, you know? It's a lot to take in," she says, referring to the fact that she had recently transferred to Blackwell Academy.

She turns to face him, feeling grateful that he's taken a seat next to her. "But I'm glad that we have this class together. It's nice to have someone to talk to," she adds, hoping to start up a friendly conversation.
"I'm getting really interested in photography recently, because of you" I say as I lean closer to her

Max smiles, her face immediately lighting up at the mention of photography. "Really? That's awesome, John. I'm glad I could inspire you to find a new interest," she says, turning to face him fully.

"What made you interested in it?" she asks curiously, her eyes shining with excitement. Max is always eager to talk about photography with anyone who is willing to listen – it's her greatest passion, after all.
I smile softly at her, "I can see how passionate you are about it, it's inspiring really"

Max feels her cheeks warm up at his words, feeling a sense of happiness and pride wash over her. "Thank you, John! I've always loved taking photos and capturing special moments. There's something magical about being able to freeze a moment in time, you know?" 

She glances down at her camera bag, which is propped up against the tree trunk next to her. "Do you want to see some of my photos later? Maybe we could go on a photo walk sometime," she suggests, hoping to share her passion with him.
"Sure, what exactly is a photo walk?" I say playful

"A photo walk is basically when we take our cameras and walk around, looking for interesting things to capture. It's a great way to practice our photography skills and explore the world around us," Max says, chuckling at John's playful tone.

"We could go down to the beach or explore some of the hidden spots around Arcadia Bay. I'm always up for an adventure," she adds with a grin.
"So basically you're asking me for a date" I say playful

Max laughs at John's remark, feeling a little embarrassed. "No, not a date, just a fun activity to do as friends," she says, playfully nudging him in the arm.

But a small part of her can't help but wonder if going on photo walks with John could turn into something more. She quickly shakes the thought away, not wanting to get ahead of herself. 

"So, do you want to do a photo walk sometime? It could be fun," she says with a smile, hoping he's as excited about it as she is.
"Sure, but I only have this as a camera, will it cut it?" I say playful as I pull out my smartphone

Max smiles, nodding her head at John's smartphone. "Of course, a smartphone camera can be just as effective as a professional camera. It's not about the equipment, it's about how you use it," she says, feeling excited about the possibility of showing John how to take great photos with his smartphone.

"I could even show you some tricks and tips on how to take better photos with your phone. It'll be fun, I promise," she adds with a wink.
"Alright, when are we going?" I say as I adjust my cap

"How about this weekend? Maybe Saturday afternoon? We could go to the beach and take some cool shots there, if that works for you," Max suggests, hoping they can make plans soon.

She feels a sense of excitement brewing inside her chest - she's always loved exploring new places to take photos, and she's looking forward to showing John some of her favorite spots.
"Alright Alright, I can’t wait to see you wearing a swimsuit" I say playful

Max chuckles, feeling a little flustered by John's comment. "Don't get too excited, John. I don't think I'll be wearing a swimsuit during our photo walk," she says, shaking her head in amusement.

"But I'm looking forward to getting some great shots with you. It's going to be a lot of fun," she adds, feeling a sense of excitement building inside her.
"Alright class is starting, let's go" I say as I get up

"Alright, let's not be late. See you later, John," Max says as she gathers her bag and follows him towards their next class.

She feels grateful for John's friendship and for his interest in photography. Maybe their photo walk will be the start of a great friendship and perhaps even more. Only time will tell.
